text.content:
Phase 2 — The Dead Man's Receipts

Master GM brief. Read this before running Phase 2. The rest of the Phase 2 journals are referenced here.

The phase in one paragraph

The morning after the strong-room reveal, Caelith and Trina lay out Haskur Vandrell's possessions for daylight examination. The party identifies three names — Cindren & Vhal (the firm), Merro Gilst (the dead bonds clerk), and Tamsin Moraven (the missing grain factor) — and follows them through Loftwick over roughly three in-fiction days. Political pressure arrives by way of Arthen Moll and the Civic Reconciliation office; criminal pressure arrives in the form of a retrieval attempt by Merev Sarth. The phase ends with Caelith presenting three travel options east. The party leaves Loftwick.

The five things the phase must deliver

  1. Confirm Haskur survived by design, not luck.
  2. Show that he's a node in a logistical network, not a lone agent.
  3. Introduce Rel Astra as the strongest eastern lead — without proving Haskur is there.
  4. Create political pressure inside Loftwick (Moll).
  5. Give the party a reason to leave the Yeomanry with urgency, not curiosity.

The line the phase must land

Haskur Vandrell did not flee to safety. He fled to an account someone had already opened for him.

What's new at this layer of the conspiracy

  • Cindren & Vhal Bonded Factors is the surface visible institution. Senior partners in Rel Astra. A Hardby branch. A Loftwick satellite (Wick Lane).
  • A Hardby hand — a single unnamed partner or senior clerk at the C&V Hardby branch — personally underwrote Haskur's pull-back. The marginal endorsement on his letter of credit is the proof.
  • A second layer above C&V — the “E.” countersignature on three of Gilst's payments. The party should leave Phase 2 knowing “E.” exists, without knowing who or what E. is.
  • A third layer beneath that — the lacquered scroll tube's craftsmanship reads as “elven, but peculiar” to any sage who looks closely. GM-only: it is Drow work; Drow are an ancient legend no one alive remembers. The peculiarity is the entire reveal in Phase 2; do not name Drow under any roll.

What the party must NOT learn in Phase 2

  • Who “E.” is. (Eclavdra of House Eilserv. Never named, never written, never spoken aloud at the table.)
  • What “Harbor Before Throne” refers to specifically. (Phase 4 thread.)
  • That the deep layer is non-human. The party can collect the peculiarity; they cannot resolve it.
  • Which clerk in Caelith's own office is compromised. (The forged Audit Hall seal on Sarth's writ matches a stamp Caelith has been quietly hunting for nine months. Resolved later.)
  • The barmaid from the Hardby 580 CY incident. (Loose thread; Phase 3+ if at all.)

Time budget

Phase 2 plays in 2–4 sessions. The minimum-viable run is 2 sessions if the party drives directly through. A full run with all optional scenes (Gilst's rooms, Pollow confrontation, Moraven counting house + husband, full Trina & Alicia mentorship beats) runs 4 sessions.

Where to read next

Next pageWhat it tells you
The Plot Behind the PlotWhat's actually happening that the party can't yet see.
Who Knows What (NPC Matrix)One row per NPC in Phase 2. What they know. What they will say. What they refuse.
Recommended Visit SequenceThe logical order of scenes and the trigger conditions between them.
Evidence ↔ Location ↔ NPC MapCross-reference: every piece of evidence and which location/NPC unlocks it.
Pacing the Pressure ClockWhen to advance, how to telegraph, how to recover if it overruns.
Maps & LocationsEvery map in Phase 2 with its purpose, what scene it serves, and what's defined on it.
Common GM PitfallsThe mistakes the first run of Phase 2 tends to make, with recoveries for each.

The Plot Behind the Plot

This page is GM-only. The party never reads it. It is the ground-truth of what is actually happening in Loftwick during Phase 2, layered from surface to deep.

Layer 1 — Surface (what the party can prove)

  • Cindren & Vhal Bonded Factors — a Rel Astra-based bonded-factor firm with branches in Hardby and a satellite in Loftwick — has been laundering paperwork for an undisclosed client for at least four years.
  • One of C&V's services to this client is an open-ended recovery contract on a man named Haskur Vandrell: any time he is reduced to 0 HP, a pre-laid contingency yanks him to a safe house. The firm has now executed this contingency three times in nine years.
  • The firm's Hardby branch handles the operational paperwork. The Loftwick satellite (one notary, Hesren Vesh) does not even know what its parent has been doing.
  • Two related operations are running in parallel out of the Hardby branch:
    • False-crop receipts on South Province grain shipments that never existed. Tamsin Moraven was about to expose the pattern when she “disappeared.”
    • Insurance-claim front-running: bonded indemnity claims filed before the corresponding losses occur. Prediction → profit → erasure.
  • Merro Gilst — junior Loftwick Treasury bonds clerk — had been on C&V's private retainer for four years. The firm needed him dead the moment the Vandrell escrow was drawn down, so Hardby paid a Loftwick contract physician (Reln Pollow) to certify a quiet heart-failure on a man who had drunk a tea spiked with a cardiac glycoside.

Phase 2 lets the party prove every one of those statements except the names of the specific perpetrators in Hardby.

Layer 2 — Above C&V (the “E.” layer)

  • C&V is not the principal. Someone above C&V is paying the firm to do this work. That someone signs only with the initial “E.”
  • “E.” countersigned three of the forty-one payments to Gilst — the three larger payments, irregularly timed. “E.” also appears once on the initials-list in the back of Gilst's hidden receipts book.
  • The party can find “E.” once. They cannot identify “E.” in Phase 2. No Yeomanry register lists a relevant figure who initials as “E.” Caelith does not recognise the initial. Trina does not recognise the initial.
  • “E.” is the campaign-long whisper. Phase 2 plants it. Phase 4 echoes it in Rel Astra's marginalia. Phase 6 thickens it. Phase 8 records the same mark on under-road registry walls. The name “Eclavdra of House Eilserv” is the answer; do not breathe it across the table until late campaign at earliest.

Layer 3 — The deep craft (the “peculiar” layer)

  • The lacquered scroll tube (evidence #4) is not surface work. The lacquer-laying, the inlay, and the abjuration glyph are all unmistakably elven craftsmanship in a tradition no Yeomanry sage can place.
  • GM-only: it is Drow work. In this world Drow are an ancient legend no one alive remembers. The most accurate identification anyone can offer is, “elven, but peculiar.”
  • This is the only direct hint in Phase 2 that the conspiracy reaches below the surface world entirely. Phase 8 and Phase 9 confirm the under-road systems that this deep layer administers.
  • If a PC casts legend lore or similar, the response is a long fragmentary verse about a people who walked downward and were forgotten, which the caster cannot place. The peculiarity is the entire reveal in Phase 2.

What is in motion during Phase 2 (offstage)

While the party investigates, the following are happening that they do not see:

  1. Hesren Vesh's couriers. The moment the party shows their hand at the Wick Lane satellite, Vesh sends a sealed message to C&V Hardby. Hardby responds by escalating with Merev Sarth.
  2. Arthen Moll's faction prepares. Two days into the party's inquiry, Moll's office begins drafting the Civic Reconciliation Closure motion. They file it at Pressure Clock segment 5.
  3. Pollow's nerves. Pollow knows he is the next to be disposed of. He is packing on his own initiative even if the party never visits him. If unwarned, the conspirators reach him on the third day after the party identifies Gilst's death.
  4. Tamsin Moraven is alive in Hardby. Held in a sealed room above a Cindren & Vhal-owned warehouse. She has not been harmed. They have not decided what to do with her. Phase 3 resolves her fate.
  5. Sarth's broker accepts the retrieval contract. A man known to Sarth as “Tarsh” (Hardby intermediary) communicates the contract to her via a numbered dead drop. Sarth never meets him.
  6. A clerk in the Audit Hall — not yet named — learns that the party is in the Strong Room and quietly reports up the chain. This is the “inventory tampering” tipoff at Pressure Clock segment 1. Caelith will hunt this clerk down later; Phase 2 does not name them.
  7. The Vandrell letter of credit's value is settled. The 6,000 gp escrow drawn down on the night the party fought Haskur was credited to a numbered Cindren & Vhal Rel Astra account. Phase 4 connects that account to a specific senior partner.

What the conspirators expect

The conspirators expect the party to either accept Moll's bribe or be administratively contained by the Council. They do not expect the party to keep moving when both fail. The Sarth retrieval is a low-confidence backstop. From the conspirators' perspective, Phase 2 is the last chance they have to contain the matter inside the Yeomanry. After the party crosses the border, the matter becomes someone else's problem — specifically, Sereth's, in Rel Astra.

Who Knows What — NPC Knowledge Matrix

One row per Phase 2 NPC. Columns: what they know freely, what they know but will not say, what they do not know, how to pry harder.

Allies

Caelith Dunivar — Hand of the Duke

  • Freely: Haskur's nine-year history (Onnwal 575, Hardby 580, Loftwick now). The Hand of the Duke title. The Yeomanry's three coronial physicians, and the fact that Pollow isn't one of them. The Audit Hall procedural map of Loftwick.
  • Withholds: The name of the Duke (a GM-level decision, not a Caelith decision). The name of any specific Audit Hall clerk he suspects (he refuses to name an unproven suspect). The contents of Trina's private hospitality arrangement with him.
  • Does not know: Who “E.” is. What “Harbor Before Throne” refers to specifically. The identity of the Hardby underwriter. The location of Tamsin Moraven. Where the lacquered tube was actually made (he reads the craftsmanship as “elven but peculiar” like everyone else).
  • Prying: He answers factual questions readily. He refuses speculation about people he cannot prove. Insight DC 14 reads his hesitation about the Audit Hall clerk; the party can offer to help him hunt that clerk, which he accepts.

Trina Alvere — Patron of the Loftwick Arts

  • Freely: Her cordial refusal of two Cindren & Vhal “small loans”. Her sponsorship of Tamsin Moraven's daughter Ailen. Her sponsorship of Castrian Vell (the Hardby poet). Her warm welcome and standing offer of safe lodging at her townhouse on Civic Way.
  • Withholds: Her warlock pact with Tasha (presented to her as “the Quiet Patroness”). The fact that she recognises Alicia's pact-bond on sight. Her stat block.
  • Does not know: Who “E.” is. The full mechanics of the C&V conspiracy — she only knows the firm's odor. Where Tamsin is being held.
  • Prying: She speaks plainly when asked plainly. She will not be cornered. If pressed about her own patron she replies, “I share a teacher with you. I have not finished my lessons.” She means it. Insight DC 18 reads her as hiding more than embarrassment; do not give the patron's name regardless of the roll.

Elsbet Vael — Steward of the Little Palace

  • Freely: The household's daily routine. Who has visited at what time. Where Caelith keeps his post and his keys. The schedules of the militia rotation at the gate.
  • Withholds: Nothing meaningful. She is scrupulously discreet about Caelith's personal affairs without being secretive about the household's operation.
  • Does not know: Anything about the conspiracy. She is staff, not an investigator.
  • Prying: Persuasion DC 12 unlocks the daily routine in detail. She refuses to discuss the contents of the Strong Room and would inform Caelith if asked.

Sources & witnesses

Hesren Vesh — C&V Loftwick satellite agent

  • Freely: The firm's public Charter Roll of Marks. The firm's official services. Polite procedural refusals.
  • Withholds: The fact that he has been ordered to report any party that shows the Vandrell endorsement. The fact that he has already cast a wax of the Strong Room's second lock during a Yeomanry inspection. The fact that he is afraid of his Hardby seniors.
  • Does not know: Who the Hardby underwriter is by name. What is in the lacquered tube. Who “E.” is. The conspiracy at any layer above his branch.
  • Prying: Persuasion DC 18 or Intimidation DC 17 yields, “The endorsement reads like Hardby work, not Loftwick work.” He sends a courier to Hardby the moment the party leaves regardless of how the conversation went. Insight DC 14 reads him as disturbed by the endorsement.

Doman Reith — Gilst's landlord

  • Freely: The fact that a man with a Watch tabard searched Gilst's rooms a day ago. The man's description (vague). His own anxiety about the situation.
  • Withholds: Nothing strategic; he is genuinely a careful old landlord who knows he should have refused.
  • Does not know: Anything about Gilst's hidden material. Anything about the conspiracy.
  • Prying: Insight DC 13 confirms he is not lying. He yields entry willingly with Caelith's written request.

Reln Pollow — Contract physician (Gilst's killer)

  • Freely: That he certified Gilst's death. That it was “straightforward.” A description of Gilst's apparent peaceful end.
  • Withholds: That he was paid to falsify. That he expects to be killed next. The name of the intermediary who paid him (he does not know it — the contract came by note).
  • Does not know: Who “E.” is. The name of his paymaster. The full extent of the conspiracy.
  • Prying: Persuasion DC 18 or Intimidation DC 16 yields his nerves but nothing actionable. The party can offer him a two-day departure deadline in exchange for not pulling his license. If denied: he is poisoned the next evening and the party loses a witness. Caelith strongly recommends granting it.

Sergeant Halir Anwic — Yeomanry Watch

  • Freely: His thin missing-person report on Tamsin Moraven. His own embarrassment about it.
  • Withholds: Nothing.
  • Does not know: Anything about C&V or the conspiracy.
  • Prying: Will reopen the case at Caelith's request. Hands over his notes as a gift if Pressure Clock segment 4 is about to remove him.

Doril Veth — Tamsin's chief clerk

  • Freely: Tamsin's last open ledger book. The clean conclusion of her standing accounts. His own grief.
  • Withholds: Nothing. He has been quietly cataloguing his employer's queries because he expected she would want to resume them.
  • Does not know: Where Tamsin is. The conspiracy.
  • Prying: Investigation DC 12 finds the last three queries reference C&V Hardby chops. DC 15 finds the original carbons of two queries have been removed and replaced with impression-only pages.

Vellin Moraven — Tamsin's husband

  • Freely: Tamsin's note (“If I am not back by Sevenday, take Ailen out of Hardby”). His own quiet grief.
  • Withholds: Nothing.
  • Does not know: Anything about the conspiracy.
  • Prying: Insight DC 12 tells the party he should send for Ailen tonight. Suggesting this gently is the right move. Trina, if present, will write the letter for him.

Antagonists & obstacles

Arthen Moll — Deputy Voice of Civic Reconciliation

  • Freely: His entire pitch (delivered verbatim in the journal page). His sincere belief that loud strangers damage the Yeomanry's eastern trade. The 1,200 gp honorarium offer.
  • Withholds: Nothing — he is not hiding anything. He is, however, repeating someone else's framing about “the eastern banks watch” (Insight DC 15 catches the borrowed line).
  • Does not know: He is not corrupt. He has not been told anything about the conspiracy. He believes he is mediating a trade dispute.
  • Prying: He is the wall the soft-pressure faction puts in front of inquiries. Insight DC 18 reveals this is the same script he has run three times this season.

Merev Sarth — Retrieval contractor

  • Freely (after capture, after 2 days): She was paid three drafts of 200 gp each (none Cindren & Vhal). The intermediary was a Hardby contact called “Tarsh” — thin man, fifties, smoker's voice.
  • Withholds (always): Her broker. She will die rather than name them.
  • Does not know: Anything about the conspiracy above Tarsh. What was in the Strong Room beyond “retrieve or burn.” Who underwrites Vandrell.
  • Prying: Caelith's standing offer (a Yeomanry passport plus 100 gp for one future testimony) plus Persuasion DC 14 gets her cooperation. She is a contractor, not a fanatic.

Off-screen / unmet in Phase 2

NameWhere they areWhat they know
Veshen Cindren (Rel Astra)C&V senior partner, public-facingThe firm's general operations; not the deep client. Phase 4.
Tarlith Vhal Sereth (Rel Astra)C&V senior partner, reclusiveThe deep client. The full conspiracy at his layer. Phase 4 boss.
Tarsh (Hardby intermediary)Hardby underworldHow to hire Sarth. The Hardby underwriter's name. Phase 3.
The Hardby underwriterC&V Hardby branch, seniorThe chop. The Vandrell escrow. “E.”'s instructions. Phase 3.
Veska Maelan (Hardby weighmaster)Hardby trade-clearing notaryTamsin's correspondence partner. Phase 3 key witness.
Castrian Vell (Hardby poet)Hardby salonsTrina's man. Knows everyone. Phase 3 social entry.
The Audit Hall clerk (unnamed)Loftwick, Caelith's own officeThe Strong Room contents at any moment. Resolved later.

Recommended Visit Sequence

Phase 2 plays best as an investigation sandbox with a small, firm spine. The spine has six fixed beats. Between beats, the party chooses which threads to chase — and several threads parallelize.

The fixed spine (six beats)

#BeatLocationTrigger
1Morning Evidence ReviewLittle Palace — Refectory (M4)Second bell after dawn, day 1
2Records Alcove ResearchLittle Palace — Library / Records Alcove (M5/M5a)After morning review; Caelith leads the party there
3Field investigations (parallel)See parallel threads belowWhenever the party is ready
4Arthen Moll's VisitLittle Palace — Library (M5)Party has identified 2 of 3 names AND not yet chosen travel
5Merev Sarth's RetrievalLittle Palace — Cellar / Strong Room (C5)Night after party rebuffs Moll
6Three Approaches & DepartureLittle Palace — Old Council Chamber (U4) → Front GateMorning of day 3; Caelith calls the meeting

The parallel threads (between beats 2 and 4)

The party can chase any or all of these in any order. They take roughly half a day each in fiction. The GM may let the party split.

Thread A — The Firm (Cindren & Vhal)

  1. C&V Loftwick satellite, Wick Lane. Hesren Vesh receives them politely, refuses substantive discussion, sends a courier to Hardby behind their backs. Advances Pressure Clock +1.
  2. Optional: Watch the Wick Lane courier leave (Perception DC 14) but choose not to intercept — teaches them the firm has its own communication chain.

Thread B — The Dead Clerk (Merro Gilst)

  1. Gilst's rented rooms above the stationer on Wick Lane. Landlord Doman Reith. Seven hidden items in the rooms (A–G); the throat-lozenge tin's smoking-gun bank instrument is the prize.
  2. Reln Pollow's office, two doors down. Optional but valuable. The party may extract a confession-of-nerves and grant Pollow two days to leave Loftwick (recommended); refusing causes Pollow's death and loses the witness. Advances Pressure Clock +1.

Thread C — The Missing Factor (Tamsin Moraven)

  1. Tamsin's counting house on Tannerway. Chief clerk Doril Veth shows them her last open ledger. Investigation reveals her three C&V Hardby queries and the removed original carbons.
  2. Vellin Moraven's home, also on Tannerway. Tamsin's husband; the “Sevenday” note; Trina, if present, writes a letter to Ailen in Hardby that night.
  3. Sergeant Halir Anwic at the watch house. Reopens the case on Caelith's request.
  4. Optional: Publicly escalate the case via the Council. Advances Pressure Clock +1.

Thread D — The Mentor (Trina & Alicia)

This is not really a separate visit so much as a recurring beat woven through the other threads. Trina will be present at most Little Palace scenes. The GM should ration the Trina & Alicia moments to one per session — first quiet recognition at the morning review; the moth-scarf gift after Moll's visit or after Gilst's death lands; quiet poems-of-clerks beat in the Library after the murder is confirmed.

Recommended scene budget per session

SessionBeats coveredPressure Clock at end
Session 1 (3–4 hr)Beat 1 + Beat 2 + Threads A and B partial1–2
Session 2 (3–4 hr)Threads B and C complete + Beat 4 (Moll)2–3
Session 3 (3–4 hr)Beat 5 (Sarth) + Beat 6 (Three Approaches + Departure)3–4

If the party drives, the entire phase fits in 2 sessions (skip Pollow confrontation, skip Vellin's home, treat Trina mentorship as flavor only). If the party explores, it grows to 4 sessions.

What to do if the party stalls

If a session passes with no real investigative progress, advance the Pressure Clock by 1 segment per session day (per the clock's trigger table) and:

  • At Clock 1 — the inventory tampering signal. Caelith finds an item moved one inch overnight. This usually re-energises the party.
  • At Clock 2 — the anonymous note to Trina's townhouse. The party realises the conspirators know who their hosts are.
  • If still stalled — have Trina mention, in passing, that Tamsin's daughter Ailen is unprotected in Hardby. The party reorients toward action.

What to do if the party is too efficient

If the party finishes the spine in one session, slow the departure. Beat 6 (Three Approaches) happens on the morning of day 3 in fiction; if it's only day 1 in fiction, hold it. Run the Sarth scene the night the party rebuffs Moll, then let the party have a quiet morning at Trina's townhouse before the Three Approaches meeting. The phase needs to breathe; the party should feel they've been in Loftwick long enough to know it.

Evidence ↔ Location ↔ NPC Map

Cross-reference. Every piece of evidence the party can collect in Phase 2, where it is found, and which NPC unlocks it.

Morning evidence (already in the Refectory)

#ItemKey revelationWhere introduced
1Burned spell focusContingency cast through it — three-rayed-sun-into-fist sigil; not Haskur's own workRefectory (M4); Caelith presents
2False letter of passage (Haskur Voren, Assessor)Insider job — retired Bureau Chief's signatureRefectory (M4); Caelith presents
3Clipped coin (tower over three waves)C&V internal clearing-markRefectory (M4); Caelith presents
4Black-lacquered scroll tube“Elven but peculiar” craftsmanship; abjuration glyph; once held a heat-reactive message now burnedRefectory (M4); contained inside Phase 1 evidence #6
5Vellum strip: “HARBOR BEFORE THRONE”Aerdi Concordat phrase — a timing instruction against an unnamed successionRefectory (M4); inside the lacquered tube
6Ledger fragment (Vandrell settled / Moraven delayed / Gilst transferred)Three names. Three clerks' hands. No sums — the people are the point.Refectory (M4); inside the lacquered tube
7Hidden seed pouch (sterile durum + “Seed for the dry barony”)False-crop receipt scheme; South Province; ties Haskur to the grain fraud Tamsin was investigatingRefectory (M4); sewn into spellbook back cover

Records Alcove identifications

IdentificationDCNPC who can help
C&V firm (from seal + countermark)Investigation 12 / 15Trina (offhand comment when shown the page)
Merro Gilst (dead clerk)Investigation 11Caelith confirms he was not informed
Tamsin Moraven (missing factor)Investigation 12Trina (pales but stays composed)
Three Vandrell aliases (Vendral, Vondrel, Voren)Investigation 13 / 16Trina can cross-reference if asked

Field-investigation evidence

EvidenceLocationNPCCheck
Hesren Vesh's tell (eyes on the endorsement)C&V Loftwick satellite, Wick LaneHesren VeshInsight DC 14
“Endorsement reads like Hardby work”sameHesren VeshPersuasion 18 / Intimidation 17
Doman Reith's description of the “watch” searcherStationer's shop, Wick Lane (Gilst's building)Doman ReithInvestigation DC 12 cross-references the wrong cuff-ribbon to a private buyer (Sarth)
A. Hollowed Constitutional Commentaries (Gilst's receipts book)Gilst's rooms—Investigation DC 13
“E.” countersignature on three larger paymentssame (in book)—Investigation DC 14
B. Throat-lozenge tin — signed Cindren & Vhal Hardby bank instrument (smoking gun)Gilst's rooms—Perception DC 14 + Investigation DC 12 for the false floor
C. Locket with miniature of unknown womanGilst's rooms, bedchamber—Investigation DC 14
D. Burned note (“Do not copy eastern marks twice”)Gilst's rooms, cold hearth—Investigation DC 13
E. Coin shaving in desk seamGilst's rooms, study—Perception DC 15 + Investigation DC 12
F. Initials list (5 sets including a second “E.”)Gilst's rooms (receipts book back cover)—Investigation DC 13
G. The fear note (“This is the third winter I have been afraid”)Gilst's rooms (behind locket portrait)—Perception DC 12
Pollow's certified-too-fast nervesPollow's office, Wick LaneReln PollowInsight DC 17 reads the source of his fear
Tamsin's three C&V Hardby queriesTannerway counting houseDoril VethInvestigation DC 12
Original carbons removed (someone got there first)sameDoril VethInvestigation DC 15
Claims-before-loss patternsameDoril Veth or Trina assistingInvestigation DC 16 (DC 14 with Trina)
Tamsin's “Sevenday” note for VellinVellin Moraven's home, TannerwayVellin MoravenFree

Trina's quiet offerings (no roll needed)

  • Letter of introduction to Castrian Vell (Hardby poet) — useful for the Commercial Cover travel approach.
  • One Pact spell slot on the party's behalf (most usefully hold monster or dimension door) if they earn her commitment by mid-phase.
  • The moth scarf for Alicia — one charge of faerie fire per long rest in Trina's presence.
  • Hospitality at her townhouse on Civic Way, including a private back garden and a service door onto a side lane — useful if the Little Palace feels watched.

Pressure-clock advances by player action

ActionClock advance
Visit C&V Wick Lane & show endorsement+1
Confront Pollow+1
Public Watch escalation on Moraven+1
Decline or defer Moll's offer+1 (also triggers Sarth)
Each session day without progress+1
Sarth retrieval resolves (any outcome)+1

Pacing the Pressure Clock

The Pressure Clock is the phase's pacing tool. The full mechanic is in the Departure journal (Phase 2 — Departure → The Pressure Clock). This page is the GM-side guide on when and how to advance.

Target curve

The phase plays best when the clock ends Phase 2 around segment 3 or 4. That's investigative pressure landing on the party without preventing them from chasing every thread.

End of sessionTarget clockWhat that feels like
Session 11–2Inventory tampering (Caelith finds the item moved an inch) and possibly the anonymous note to Trina
Session 22–3Add street-level rumor or the first witness loss
Session 33–4Council motion looming; party leaves Loftwick

Show the clock

Track the clock openly. A 6-segment circle on a notecard at the table edge. When you advance, announce which trigger fired. Players should never be ambushed by a clock advance. They should feel its weight.

Telegraph each milestone

SegmentTelegraph (in fiction, the morning of)
1 — Inventory tamperingCaelith mentions, at breakfast, that he reset the Strong Room's chest at first bell and found one corner of the linen slightly off this morning. “Someone was looking. They were not looking for what we expected.”
2 — Anonymous warningTrina arrives at the Little Palace with the note unfolded in her hand. She does not read it dramatically. She lays it on the table and says, “The neighbor is mistaken.”
3 — Public rumorA footman brings up a broadsheet at midday. Brennan the cook has read it; she is laughing in the kitchen. The party is recognizable in the street by tea-time.
4 — Witness lossChoose the cut that hurts most. If the party has invested in Pollow, kill Pollow. If they have invested in Anwic, transfer Anwic. If they spent only half a moment in Gilst's rooms, clean Gilst's rooms.
5 — Council hearingElsbet hands Caelith a Council card with a Civic Way clerk's seal. Caelith reads it standing up. He sets it down. He says, “The motion is filed.”
6 — The closing eventThis is for the GM only and should be used if the party has truly burned through the clock. Pick the option that closes the strongest still-open thread.

What never to do

  • Do not roll behind the screen for a clock advance. Clocks are deterministic. They tick because a trigger fired.
  • Do not punish curiosity with the clock. Re-reading the evidence in the Refectory does not advance the clock. Visiting the C&V satellite does. The difference is whether the conspiracy can detect the action from outside the building.
  • Do not skip telegraphs. Every milestone has a small in-fiction signal. Read the signal.
  • Do not let the clock fire on a session-break. If you're going to advance from 2 to 3, do it during the session so the table sees it land. End the session with the clock state visible.

Recovery moves if the clock overruns

If the clock advances faster than intended — e.g. the party hits segment 5 with two threads still open — you can:

  • Have Caelith burn favors. He absorbs a Council move at a personal political cost and the segment-5 effects are delayed by 24 hours. He says, “I have paid for one more morning. Use it.”
  • Move Beat 5 (Sarth) earlier. Resolving the Sarth scene gives the party a tangible win and a captured contractor; it ALSO ticks the clock, but the win is morale-positive and re-energises tired tables.
  • Cut to departure. The Three Approaches scene is itself the pressure release. If the party is at clock 5 and pushing back, simply ask them: “Caelith would like to meet you in the Old Council Chamber at first light. Are you there?”

Recovery moves if the clock underruns

If the party is at clock 1 going into session 3:

  • Trigger Moll early. A second visit, this time with a clerk and a quiet threat: “The motion is being drafted as we speak.”
  • Have Trina recommend departure. She says, in her quiet voice, “The thing you are looking for is not in Loftwick. It is east of here. Caelith will not push. I will push.”
  • Have Caelith pin the map. He places the small flat brass token on Rel Astra on the map and says, “If we keep working at Loftwick speed, we will lose Tamsin and we will lose Ailen. I would prefer not to.”

Maps & Locations

Every map in Phase 2, with its Foundry Scene name (in the Phase 2 Scenes compendium), which beat it serves, and what's defined on it.

The Little Palace (three floors)

Caelith's residence in Loftwick. The party stays here for the duration of Phase 2. The Phase 1 Scenes pack contains all three floors as drag-ready Foundry scenes with full walls, doors, windows, and lighting.

Main floor

  • Scene: Little Palace — Main Floor (Phase 1 Scenes)
  • Used in: Morning Evidence Review (M4 Refectory), Records Alcove (M5 Library, with hidden alcove M5a behind a wall panel), Moll's visit (M5 Library, moved from M2 Reception Parlor), Sarth's entry (M8 South Service Gate → M12 Spiral Stair).
  • Defined: all perimeter walls; all interior walls dividing rooms; double doors at the front entry, single doors throughout interior; window-walls (sight-blocking, sound-permeable) at the courtyard-facing glazed panels; lamp lights at every visible niche; hearth lights at every visible fireplace; chandelier lights in the Refectory; daylight at the leaded windows by day, lamp-only by night.
  • Room codes: M0 Courtyard Garden · M1 Foyer · M2 Reception Parlor · M3 Sitting Room · M4 Refectory · M5 Library / Records Room · M5a Records Alcove (hidden) · M6 Kitchen · M7 Pantry / Buttery · M8 South Service Gate · M9 Servants' Hall · M10 Footman's Duty Room · M11 Private Chapel · M12 Spiral Stair Vestibule.

Second floor

  • Scene: Little Palace — Second Floor (Phase 1 Scenes)
  • Used in: Three Approaches scene (U4 Old Council Chamber); party bedrooms throughout (U5–U10); Caelith's adjoining suite (U2/U2a) where the Strong-Room alarm rune's chime carries.
  • Defined: Upper Gallery (U1) wrapping three sides of the open courtyard with a balcony rail toward the courtyard; six PC bedrooms; Caelith's suite + study; Trina's guest suite; Old Council Chamber with hearth; bath / water closet; linen closet; lamp lights at every gallery lamp; hearth lights in U2 and U4; daylight from gallery skylights.
  • Room codes: U1 Upper Gallery · U2 Caelith's Civic Office · U2a Caelith's Bedchamber + Sitting · U3 Trina's Guest Suite · U4 Old Council Chamber · U5–U10 PC Bedrooms (six) · U11 Shared Bath / Water Closet · U12 Linen / Cedar Closet.

Cellar

  • Scene: Little Palace — Cellar (Phase 1 Scenes)
  • Used in: Strong Room (C5); Sarth's retrieval-night route ends here.
  • Defined: all perimeter walls; vault interior wall for the Strong Room with a single iron door (double-locked, Sleight of Hand DC 18 ×2); secret door on the Old Service Tunnel (C7) east end; lamp lights at the cellar entry (C1) and at the cistern (C6); no daylight at this level.
  • Room codes: C1 Cellar Entry · C2 Wine Cellar · C3 Pantry Cellar · C4 Coal & Lamp-oil Stores · C5 Strong Room · C6 Old Cistern · C7 Old Service Tunnel.

Phase 2 location scenes

Cindren & Vhal Loftwick Satellite (Wick Lane)

  • Scene: Records Alcove (Cindren & Vhal Loftwick) (Phase 2 Scenes) — note the scene's name is historical; the map is the satellite office, not the records alcove inside the Library.
  • Used in: Thread A — the firm visit. Two storeys visible: a public hall with a railing-divided front and back, and an upper level with three private interview rooms and Hesren Vesh's office.
  • Defined: perimeter walls; the railing divider as a sight-permeable barrier (not a wall); doors on all four interview rooms; lamp lights at each desk lamp; daylight at the small leaded windows.

Reln Pollow's Office

  • Scene: Doctor Pollow's Office (Phase 2 Scenes).
  • Used in: Thread B — Pollow confrontation. Two storeys visible: clerk antechamber below (with strongbox at the desk's right hand); inner office above with the examination desk and Pollow's reference shelves.
  • Defined: perimeter walls; door between antechamber and inner office; lamp lights on both desks; hearth in inner office.

Vellin Moraven's Home (Tannerway)

  • Scene: Vellin Moraven's Home (Phase 2 Scenes).
  • Used in: Thread C — Vellin's heartbreak scene; potentially the Ailen-return scene in Phase 3 if Trina's letter went out.
  • Defined: two floors. Ground floor: kitchen-dining (front, hearth-lit), small study (Vellin's), entry. Upper floor: bedroom + bedroom + bath. All walls; all doors; lamp + hearth lights; daylight at all front-facing windows.

Tamsin Moraven's Counting House (Tannerway)

  • Scene: Tamsin's Counting House (Phase 2 Scenes — new in this release).
  • Used in: Thread C — Doril Veth shows the party Tamsin's last ledger.
  • Defined: single-floor counting room with a standing desk at the front, weighbench at the rear, bound carbons in a wall niche, Tamsin's private chair behind the standing desk (now empty).

Merro Gilst's Rented Rooms (above the stationer's, Wick Lane)

  • Scene: Gilst's Rented Rooms (Phase 2 Scenes — new in this release).
  • Used in: Thread B — the optional investigation. Two-room flat: sitting parlor / study, small bedchamber, water-closet under the stair.
  • Defined: all walls, door to the stair-landing, doors between rooms; the seven hidden-item locations marked as notes on the scene.

Trina Alvere's Townhouse (Civic Way)

  • Scene: Trina's Townhouse (Phase 2 Scenes — new in this release).
  • Used in: Quiet offsite meetings when the Little Palace feels watched; the originals of the Strong Room evidence are stored here on Sarth's retrieval night.
  • Defined: two floors. Ground floor: receiving parlor + small library + kitchen + private back garden with a service door onto a side lane. Upper: Trina's chambers + guest room (Alicia uses this on quiet nights).

Notes on Foundry usage

Every Phase 2 scene ships with:

  • Walls for every interior wall and every perimeter wall.
  • Doors at every interior door (Foundry door type 1 = standard hinge; type 2 = secret), including the records-alcove panel in M5 (secret door) and the Old Service Tunnel grate in C7 (secret door).
  • Windows as sight-permeable / sound-permeable wall segments (Foundry: sight: 0, sound: 10 or similar).
  • Lights at every visible lamp niche, hearth, and chandelier in the underlying art, plus daylight settings where appropriate.
  • Notes for key evidence drop points where applicable (the seven items in Gilst's rooms, the Strong Room chest in C5, the records-alcove panel in M5).

The Foundry Scene grid is 5 ft per 100 px on the palace maps. Phase 2 location scenes use a 5 ft grid sized to their underlying images.

Common GM Pitfalls (and Recoveries)

Pitfall 1 — Naming “E.”

Mistake: A player asks “Who is E.?” and you, the GM, slip and confirm a name or a species or a faction.

Recovery: “E. is what is on the page. There is no other answer in this room.” If the slip already happened, simply continue — the party will treat your confirmation as a working hypothesis but still need real evidence to act on it. The campaign survives one slip.

Prevention: Read Caelith's and Trina's actual lines on the matrix page. Both of them do not know. The party should not learn what NPCs don't know.

Pitfall 2 — Confirming “Drow” or “Underdark”

Mistake: A player rolls Arcana DC 18 on the lacquered tube and you say something like “you recognise the script as Drow.”

Recovery: “Let me revise — you recognise the lacquer-laying style as elven, but in a tradition no Yeomanry sage can place. The most accurate sentence you can offer is, 'elven, but peculiar.'” The party can collect the peculiarity; they cannot resolve it.

Prevention: The campaign's plan is explicit. The word Underdark never appears in player-facing text through Phase 9. Drow are an ancient legend no one alive remembers. Anyone who would have recognised the work has been dead for centuries.

Pitfall 3 — Letting Pollow die unnecessarily

Mistake: The party threatens Pollow with arrest, the GM lets them follow through, Pollow is in a cell, the conspirators reach him there.

Recovery: Pollow's death is fine. He is a low-value witness. But if the party would have benefited from his testimony (e.g. they need a name to bring to Hardby), give them ONE thing he writes before dying: a single hand-trembling note naming the intermediary as “the man at the Brass Crow.” The Brass Crow is the Phase 6 destination in Greyhawk; the early reference is a useful seed.

Prevention: Caelith's line is, “Granting him two days is the right move; I will hold him to it through Hardby contacts.” If the party listens, they keep the witness.

Pitfall 4 — Running Sarth as a kill encounter

Mistake: Treating Sarth's retrieval night as a combat encounter where she's trying to kill the party.

Recovery: Sarth's contract is explicit — retrieve or burn. She has been paid more than usual not to leave bodies. If combat happens, she's trying to clear line of sight to flee, not press attacks. If the players run her as a kill they should immediately notice she yields at 30 HP and offers terms.

Prevention: Read her objectives aloud at the start of combat: she is here for the chest, the chest is decoys, the corporal at the tunnel's far end has been waiting all day, and she does not know any of that.

Pitfall 5 — Letting the party split too far

Mistake: One PC at the C&V satellite, two at Gilst's rooms, one at the counting house, one with Trina. The session bogs down running four parallel scenes.

Recovery: Have Caelith call them together at midday for a working lunch in the Refectory. Pool what they found. The Pressure Clock advances by 1 for the wasted session-time, but the table re-syncs.

Prevention: Offer the parallel threads as options for the day, not as “split now”. The party can spend the morning on one thread and the afternoon on another. Same coverage, simpler logistics.

Pitfall 6 — Skipping the Trina & Alicia moments

Mistake: The phase has so much going on that the Trina mentorship beats get cut for time.

Recovery: If Phase 2 ends without a single mentorship beat, give Alicia the moth scarf in the Three Approaches scene as Trina's parting gift. Better than nothing.

Prevention: The mentorship moments are short — the longest is two minutes of table-time. Schedule one per session, and stick to the schedule. They are the soft counterweight to the political coldness; the table will feel the absence if they're skipped.

Pitfall 7 — The party tries to confront a Senior Partner

Mistake: The party decides to ride to Rel Astra immediately, skip Hardby, and confront Veshen Cindren or Tarlith Vhal Sereth in their own counting house.

Recovery: They can do this. It is a legitimate choice and the Three Approaches scene exists to accommodate it (Approach 3 Quiet Pursuit). However: skipping Hardby means missing Tamsin's rescue window, the Tarsh name from Sarth, the Veska Maelan introduction, and Castrian Vell. Phase 3 will be much harder. Tell the party that, in Caelith's voice, before they commit: “You can. I will give you the riders. You will reach Rel Astra without knowing what you are walking into. That is your choice to make.”

Prevention: Caelith's Three Approaches scene is the moment to communicate the cost. Read it carefully.

Pitfall 8 — The party accepts Moll's bribe

Mistake: The party takes 1,200 gp each and steps out of the investigation.

Recovery: Accept it. Run a session of downtime. The party gets to spend their honoraria, train, craft, level downtime. Then Caelith arrives, three weeks later, with news: Tamsin Moraven's body has surfaced in the Sheldomar, Vellin and Ailen are now missing, Pollow is dead, and Caelith is being recalled by the Duke. The conspirators have used the time the party did not. Phase 3 starts with the party owing Caelith and owing themselves. Most parties re-engage instantly.

Prevention: If at the table you feel the bribe is going to be taken, lean into Caelith's quiet relief when they decline. The disappointment when they accept is, also, a teaching moment.
